subject: phux-7ubw
message: Client mirroring/letterbox: render a referenced Terminal letterboxed when view size differs

bead.title: Client mirroring/letterbox: render a referenced Terminal letterboxed when view size differs
bead.description:
ADR-0027 (decision points 1-2). When multiple views reference one Terminal and a view's slot is larger than the Terminal's authoritative (cols, rows), render the Terminal mirrored and LETTERBOXED in that view rather than reflowing the shared grid (a Terminal has one PTY winsize / one grid width). When the chosen size is larger than a view, the view clamps. Honest, documented behavior per ADR-0027, matching tmux.

DEPENDS ON: phux-xjgs (window-size config key) — the letterbox path needs the resolved policy to know WHICH size is authoritative before it can render the others letterboxed/clamped. Also relates to phux-nk07 (multi-client viewport ownership) for the server-side geometry pick.

v1 mirrors share scroll (per-view scroll is deferred in ADR-0027).

LANE NOTE: client letterbox/mirroring rendering code — kept out of the window-size config PR by design.

Source: ADR/0027-terminal-references-and-l3-links.md, decision points 1, 2, 3 (deferred scroll).
bead.notes:
SCOPE ASSESSMENT (2026-06-07, after landing the nk07/xjgs server policy in PR #76). Two findings make this a dedicated, real-TUI-verified PR rather than a quick follow-up:

1. MIRRORING IS STRUCTURALLY UNBUILT. The headline premise ('multiple views reference one Terminal') has no consumer: crates/phux-client-core/src/multi_pane/layout.rs pane_rects() returns HashMap<TerminalId, Rect> — one slot per Terminal. The layout tree's leaves map uniquely to TerminalIds; the same Terminal cannot occupy two slots. Mirroring needs a layout-model change (allow a Terminal in N leaves) first.

2. THE REACHABLE SLICE (single-view letterbox when the policy-chosen grid < the client's slot, now possible post-nk07) requires UNWINDING the client-drives-geometry model. Today crates/phux-client/src/attach/paint.rs paint_focused_pane() calls safe_resize(slot.terminal, rect.w, rect.h) — it forces the LOCAL libghostty mirror to the slot size and renders at the rect origin, and the reflow pipeline (attach/reflow.rs) emits RESIZE to drive the PTY to that slot (rasterize.rs:64-67: 'the rect a pane is painted into always equals the rect pane_rects tells the server to size the PTY to'). To letterbox, the client must instead size its mirror to the SERVER's authoritative grid (the snapshot cols/rows the nk07 policy picked), compute a centered offset within the rect, render_at that offset, and clear the margins — i.e. defer to the server's geometry instead of owning it. That touches paint + reflow + the RESIZE-emission loop together and must be verified in a real multi-client TUI (two clients of different sizes on one session), which a headless test can't fully cover.

RECOMMEND: split into (a) single-view letterbox under the nk07 policy [reachable now, the valuable 80%], and (b) true mirroring [needs the layout-model multi-leaf change first]. (a) is the next bead to take; (b) waits on a layout-model bead. The server half it depended on (phux-xjgs/nk07) is shipped.
SCOPED (2026-06-11, render plan): Single-view letterbox is render-only, NO wire/protocol/driver-hub changes. The mirror is ALREADY server-authoritative (server_frame.rs:271-278 is the ONE resize site; paint_focused_pane/paint_full_frame already DO NOT resize the mirror to the rect — phux-wurs landed that). Today render_at_inner (render.rs:325-481) CLIPS via rows_total=min(mirror,clip), cols_total=min(mirror,clip) (render.rs:385-386) at origin (rect.x,rect.y). Letterbox = change clip->center: when mirror dims < rect, compute centered sub-rect (pad_x=(rect.w-mirror_cols)/2, pad_y=(rect.h-mirror_rows)/2), shift CUP origin by the pad, and paint blank-fill bars in the 4 margins; when mirror > rect keep today's clip (clamp). PLAN: (P1) add a pure helper letterbox_rect(rect, mirror_cols, mirror_rows)->(inner_origin, inner_clip, margins) — unit-testable, no I/O. (P2) thread an effective_origin/effective_clip + margin-fill into render_at_inner OR add render_at_letterboxed wrapper that emits margin bars then calls the existing core at the centered origin; keep render_at(min-clip) as the clamp path. (P3) call it from paint.rs paint_focused_pane (render.rs:80-86) and paint_full_frame non-focused loop (paint.rs:178-184) using slot.terminal.cols()/rows() vs rect.w/h. (P4) margin fill: emit ' ' runs (or theme bg) for the pad rows/cols, each a CUP+blank-run; reset SGR. FILES: render.rs (+ helper +letterbox path), paint.rs (call sites, pass mirror dims). STAYS WITHIN render.rs+paint.rs — does NOT touch driver hub, reflow.rs, server_frame.rs, or layout/rasterize. TESTS (headless, in render.rs #[cfg(test)]): mirror smaller than rect -> centered (CUP offset by pad), correct N margin rows/cols blanked; mirror == rect -> no padding (byte-identical to today); mirror larger than rect -> clamps (existing clip tests stay green); odd-diff centering (floor split, extra pad on bottom/right); cursor last_cursor includes pad offset. DEPENDS-ON note unchanged: true multi-leaf mirroring (same Terminal in N slots) still needs the layout-model change (client-core multi_pane/layout.rs pane_rects HashMap<TerminalId,Rect> is 1 slot/Terminal) — OUT OF SCOPE for this render-only cut; this delivers the reachable single-view letterbox under the nk07/xjgs policy.

subject: phux-pxaj
message: [epic] Perfect predictive echo

bead.title: [epic] Perfect predictive echo
bead.description: Predictive (Mosh-class) local echo is experimental and currently being flipped OFF by default because it mispredicts in common real-world cases. Make it robust enough to re-enable by default. Known issues: (1) vi-mode shells (bindkey -v / set -o vi): normal-mode keys h/j/k/l/i/x/dd are COMMANDS not inserts, so every keystroke mispredicts an insert glyph the server then contradicts — the client can't detect vi mode without shell integration; (2) top-left ghost after fast split/focus-change (FIXED phux-cdvr: suspend until authoritative cursor); (3) general fragility under fast layout/screen transitions and alt-screen apps. Direction to evaluate: adaptive auto-back-off (stop predicting after N consecutive reconcile contradictions, resume when typing normalizes); only predict at/after a known prompt boundary; OSC-133 shell-integration signal for prompt + mode; RTT-adaptive enable. Re-enable default only once vi-mode + modal apps are clean. See phux-7ry0, phux-cdvr, phux-9gw.* lineage.
bead.notes:
SCOPED (adaptive-backoff slice): First shippable slice of self-healing predictive echo. GOAL: when the server keeps CONTRADICTING predictions (vi-mode, modal apps, fast transitions), auto-suspend predicting; resume when reconciles confirm cleanly again. Reuses the existing suspend/is_suspended/set_cursor-rearm machinery (phux-cdvr) — no new overlay/render work.

CONTRADICTION-TRACKING DESIGN (all internal, no config knob this slice; thresholds hardcoded const in state.rs, doc as future-tunable like PredictiveConfig note at state.rs:24):
- Add to PredictionState (state.rs:130 struct, after the suspended field): contradiction_streak:u32 (consecutive reconcile passes that contradicted), clean_confirm_streak:u32 (consecutive clean productive passes since auto-suspend), and auto_backed_off:bool to distinguish a BACKOFF suspend from the existing re-anchor suspend so set_cursor unconditional re-arm (state.rs:262) does NOT prematurely clear a backoff.
- New method note_reconcile(&mut self, stats: ReconcileStats) on PredictionState: if stats.contradicted>0 then contradiction_streak+=1, clean_confirm_streak=0, and when streak>=BACKOFF_THRESHOLD (const 3) call internal auto_suspend (sets suspended=true + auto_backed_off=true, drops queue like suspend). If stats.contradicted==0 AND stats.confirmed>0 (clean PRODUCTIVE pass) then clean_confirm_streak+=1, contradiction_streak=0, and when auto_backed_off && clean_confirm_streak>=REARM_THRESHOLD (const 2) clear auto_backed_off + suspended=false. Empty passes (confirmed==0 && contradicted==0) are NEUTRAL — leave streaks alone.
- ReconcileStats already has confirmed/contradicted/pending (reconcile.rs:49) and is computed by reconcile_terminal_output_per_cell. PREFER: reconcile fn calls state.note_reconcile(summary) just before return (reconcile.rs:161) so all callers get backoff free; the contradicted-suffix clear() (reconcile.rs:149) stays untouched. IMPORTANT: streak fields must NOT be reset by clear() or suspend() (clear runs on every contradiction) — keep them out of those resets.
- set_cursor guard (state.rs:262): only clear suspended when !auto_backed_off, so a backoff survives cursor/snapshot syncs and only the clean-confirm path re-arms. Existing re-anchor suspend() (state.rs:201, from reanchor_predict_to_pane) leaves auto_backed_off untouched.

DRIVER HOOK (light): server_frame.rs:528 already discards _stats from reconcile_terminal_output_per_cell. Internal approach = ZERO driver diff (keep the call). Alt: rename _stats to stats and add predict.note_reconcile(stats). Chosen: internal. The wholesale-drain reconcile_terminal_output (reconcile.rs:67) does NOT feed backoff (not a per-cell verdict) — leave it.

CONFIG: no new knob. predictive-echo (phux-config ExperimentalCfg, default false) stays the only surface; backoff is internal and only runs when echo is opted-in. Thresholds = hardcoded consts doc-earmarked for a future PredictiveConfig field. Default stays OFF; this slice makes opt-in self-healing, a later slice flips default once vi-mode clean.

TESTS (mirror reconcile.rs per-cell + state.rs unit module):
- state.rs: 3 contradictions in a row via note_reconcile then is_suspended true & predict_key Skipped; backed-off state survives set_cursor (still suspended); 2 clean productive confirms after backoff then re-armed (predict_key Predicted); empty passes do not move streaks; re-anchor suspend() independent of auto_backed_off.
- reconcile.rs: integration — predict_key inserts then reconcile with contradicting cells 3x via row_reader, assert is_suspended; then confirming cells, eventually re-armed. Reuse row_reader/key_text helpers.

FILES TOUCHED (swarm conflict map): predict/state.rs (struct fields + note_reconcile + auto_suspend + set_cursor guard + tests); predict/reconcile.rs (call note_reconcile before return + integration tests); attach/server_frame.rs (NONE if internal; else 1 line at :528). No config-crate change.

PHASING: P1 state.rs fields + note_reconcile + auto_suspend + set_cursor guard + unit tests. P2 wire into reconcile_terminal_output_per_cell + integration tests. P3 verify zero/one-line driver hook at server_frame.rs:528. P4 docs: predict/mod.rs backoff paragraph + consts as future-tunable. OUT OF SCOPE: OSC-133, RTT-adaptive, flipping default ON.

subject: phux-ign
message: phux-7vx follow-up: per-Terminal TERM as L1 SPAWN field; restore TERM=ghostty when round-trip is proven

bead.title: phux-7vx follow-up: per-Terminal TERM as L1 SPAWN field; restore TERM=ghostty when round-trip is proven
bead.description:
Today's phux-7vx fix swaps the default TERM=ghostty -> TERM=xterm-256color on the server-spawned PTY (pane_actor.rs:default_shell_command). Reason: TERM=ghostty's terminfo advertises 'fullkbd' which newer ncurses interprets as 'kitty keyboard protocol safe', so htop/less/vim/etc. push CSI > N u, libghostty pivots to CSI-u for every keypress, and TUIs that don't speak kitty (like htop) drop quit/F-keys.

Two follow-on tasks live under this:

1. PROVE THE ROUND-TRIP. Validate phux's input/output layer round-trips kitty KIP correctly for at least: htop, less, vim, neovim, fzf, btop. Integration tests using the wave-4 Screen harness (Screen helper at crates/phux-server/tests/common/screen.rs) where possible, manual smoke tests where not. Once this is green, restore TERM=ghostty default and the user gets ghostty's full terminfo capabilities.

2. EXPOSE TERM AS L1 SPAWN CONFIG. Under ADR-0015 the L1 SPAWN message creates a Terminal. The PTY env (TERM, LANG, PHUX_*, etc.) is part of that spawn. Today the server hardcodes TERM in default_shell_command. Move to a per-Terminal env-map field in the SPAWN request so a consumer (TUI or agent) can override it. Default value lives in phux-config (when that crate gets fleshed out). Blocked by L1 cascade for the wire-message shape.

Commit reference: 9734d42 (the current hardcoded swap).
bead.notes: SCOPED (file:line plan, 2026-06): Part 2 (defaults.term phux-config knob + apply_term precedence) ALREADY LANDED — schema.rs:91 term field, default_term() schema.rs:190 = xterm-256color, spawn.rs:131 DEFAULT_TERM, spawn.rs:141 apply_term, attach.rs:487-488 server applies s.term() then wire env wins. Per-spawn TERM is reachable TODAY via SPAWN_TERMINAL.env TERM entry (attach.rs:499-503, precedence doc attach.rs:478-486). REMAINING for phux-ign: first-class optional TERM field on SPAWN (additive). PHASE A wire (DISJOINT from client render): add field::spawn_terminal::TERM=6 (field.rs:239-250); add term: Option<String> to FrameKind::SpawnTerminal (frame.rs:1522-1536, enc frame.rs:1970-1997 write_field id 6 when Some, dec decode.rs:849-885 new arm, default None — _=>{} loop already forward-compat). FrameKind is #[non_exhaustive] (frame.rs:1066) but struct-variant add breaks exhaustive matches: update constructors input_dispatch.rs:715/763 (env stays None, term None), decode.rs:878, tests frame_wire_snapshots.rs:747/761, wire_roundtrip.rs:1570/1936/1955, server tests. PHASE B server: attach.rs handle_spawn_terminal signature (432-439) take term:Option<String>; apply precedence compiled-in -> s.term() (488) -> wire term field -> wire env TERM; thread through runtime/client.rs:717. PHASE C tests: new wire snapshot w/ term field, roundtrip, server spawn test asserting cmd.get_env(TERM). phux-0o8 round-trip proof is ORTHOGONAL and BLOCKED: encoder already pivots correctly (htop_keys.rs proves legacy q=0x71 default + CSI-u after CSI>31u; client decodes inbound CSI u input.rs:55-66; downsample.rs:236-250 gates kbd replies on caps). Gap is NOT in phux — htop doesn't parse CSI-u; real-TUI proof needs htop/nvim/btop/fzf/less pinned in flake.nix (currently absent, only /bin/sh + cat are reproducible fixtures). Do NOT flip default to ghostty until phux-0o8 manual smoke passes; per-spawn term field + defaults.term already let users opt in deliberately.

subject: phux-7143
message: copy-mode: engine-driven word/line/all/semantic-output select on client

bead.title: copy-mode: engine-driven word/line/all/semantic-output select on client
bead.description: Wire copy-mode's word/line/all and semantic-prompt select-output to the client's own libghostty engine via select_word/select_line/select_all/select_output (SelectLineOptions::with_semantic_prompt_boundary), on slot.terminal. Client-local per ADR-0030. Builds on the client-local copy bead. Parent: phux-abi.
bead.notes:
SCOPED (read-only investigation, 2026-06-11):

ARCHITECTURE / BOUNDARY: Selection is a client-local projection (ADR-0030). The overlay (render/overlay/copy_mode.rs) holds NO terminal ref — only pane-local row/col + SelectionMode, and emits a coordinate-only CopyRequest {start/end row/col, rectangle}. Engine resolution lives entirely in attach/copy.rs (copy_to_host_clipboard -> extract_selection_text -> Terminal::grid_ref + Selection::new + format_selection_alloc), invoked from the input_dispatch.rs copy-mode arm (OverlayOutcome::Copy at line ~184-194) against slot.terminal. render/ never imports libghostty_vt (CI boundary guard) — keep it that way.

NOT BLOCKED ON phux-97w. 97w's dangling-pointer bug is ONLY in the Formatter{selection:Some(_)} path (Formatter::new_inner). The one-shot format_selection_alloc + FormatOptions::with_selection path is SOUND at the current pin 6d49dfd and is already what copy.rs uses (confirmed in phux-server/src/search.rs lines 39-60). select_word/select_line/select_all/select_output ALL have safe Rust wrappers on Terminal at this pin (selection.rs:201-336): select_all(), select_line(SelectLineOptions::new(ref).with_semantic_prompt_boundary(true)), select_output(grid_ref), select_word(SelectWordOptions::new(ref)). Selection exposes start()/end()/is_rectangle(). All option types public under libghostty_vt::selection::*. EVERYTHING is doable NOW.

PLAN (phased, client-only, NO wire/protocol change):
P1 verbs that DON'T need cursor grid_ref: extend SelectionMode -> add a SelectionGrab enum carried on CopyRequest (or a new OverlayCommand::Copy variant param). 'A'=select-all maps to Terminal::select_all(); engine derives Selection directly, format via existing format_selection_alloc. Add key 'A' in copy_mode.rs handle_key.
P2 verbs that NEED the cursor grid_ref (resolved in copy.rs, not overlay): 'w'=word -> select_word(SelectWordOptions::new(grid_ref(Point::Viewport{cursor_col,cursor_row}))); 'V'=line -> select_line(SelectLineOptions::new(ref)); semantic 'V' variant -> .with_semantic_prompt_boundary(true). For these the overlay must pass its cursor (row,col) through CopyRequest so copy.rs can build grid_ref(Point::Viewport) and call the derive helper instead of building a linear Selection from two corners.
P3 select-semantic-output: ']' or similar -> select_output(grid_ref at cursor). Requires OSC-133 zones in the pane's mirror; degrade to None (no-op, best-effort) when absent — matches existing blank-span no-op contract.

KEY DESIGN CHOICE: add an enum (e.g. SelectionGrab { Rect, Word, Line, LineSemantic, All, Output }) to CopyRequest replacing/augmenting the bool . copy.rs::extract_selection_text matches on it: Rect/Char keep the two-corner Selection::new path; the rest call the derive helpers with a grid_ref at (cursor_row,cursor_col) and format the returned Selection. Overlay must carry cursor coords in CopyRequest (it already has cursor_row/col).

FILES TOUCHED (stays within render/overlay/ + input_dispatch copy-mode arm + attach/copy.rs — low swarm-conflict surface):
- crates/phux-client/src/render/overlay/copy_mode.rs (add key bindings w/V/A/], extend copy_request())
- crates/phux-client/src/render/overlay/mod.rs (extend CopyRequest with grab enum + cursor coords)
- crates/phux-client/src/attach/copy.rs (match grab enum -> derive helper vs two-corner Selection)
- crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs (optional: status strip text per grab mode)
- input_dispatch.rs copy-mode arm unchanged structurally (already routes OverlayOutcome::Copy)

TEST STRATEGY: unit tests in copy.rs (extract via select_word/line/all/output using fresh Terminal + vt_write fixtures, incl. OSC-133 \x1b]133;A/B/C\x07 fixtures for semantic line + select_output — see synthesizer.rs:1680 for the fixture pattern); key-binding tests in copy_mode.rs (w/V/A produce CopyRequest with correct grab). No protocol/wire tests needed (client-local). cargo nextest run -p phux-client; clippy -D warnings.

subject: phux-shz9
message: [epic] Swarm batch — parallel backlog map-reduce

bead.title: [epic] Swarm batch — parallel backlog map-reduce
bead.description:
ENTRY POINT for a fresh-session swarm. Goal: map-reduce the phux backlog across parallel agents.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINT (learned 2026-06-10/11): client changes funnel through driver.rs / server_frame.rs / input_dispatch.rs / paint.rs / render.rs. Agents editing these in parallel collide in the REDUCE (merge) phase. So: parallelize ACROSS disjoint lanes; SERIALIZE within the client lane (rebase + just ci between each). origin/main moves under you (multiple concurrent agents in this workspace — main checkout is on other branches; NEVER touch it, always use your own worktree off origin/main).

WORKFLOW per item: worktree off origin/main -> implement -> nix develop --command just ci GREEN -> clippy+fmt preflight (backtick doc identifiers; map_or not map().unwrap_or()) -> push (gh auth switch --user phall1 first) -> PR -> watch CI -> rebase onto moving main if needed + re-run just ci -> merge -> close bead. Verify TUI render changes via emulator where possible (snapshot --cells); assembled-frame render is the one thing CI can't prove.

LANE A — Client render/UX (SERIALIZE; shared hub). Order matters:
  A1 phux-jow6 mouse-while-zoomed (quick) [turnkey: bead has fix]
  A2 phux-l5xa snapshot --rendered HARNESS [turnkey] -- DO EARLY: unblocks verified render for A3+
  A3 phux-4h5a sidebar 2/2 [turnkey: foundation merged #98/#99, integration plan on bead] -- verify with A2
  A4 phux-7143 copy-mode word/line/semantic select [scoped by agent]
  A5 phux-7ubw letterbox/mirror [scoped by agent]

subject: phux-fak5
message: Graceful server upgrade: sessions survive a binary update (ADR-0032)

bead.title: Graceful server upgrade: sessions survive a binary update (ADR-0032)
bead.description:
Per ADR-0032 (Proposed). Today a binary upgrade requires 'phux kill' -> restart, which closes every PTY master and SIGHUPs the children (shells, editors, the Claude agent session) — the maintainer hit this directly (installing a fix killed the session hosting the work). tmux has the same hole; phux can close it because it ALREADY emits a replayable per-pane snapshot (vt_replay_bytes + scrollback_bytes via SnapshotSynthesizer, ADR-0013).

APPROACH (ADR-0032): graceful in-place upgrade via execve re-exec + fd inheritance + VT snapshot replay. On 'phux upgrade' the running server (1) snapshots every pane + serializes the session/window/pane tree + an fd->pane manifest into an inherited state fd; (2) clears FD_CLOEXEC on the PTY masters + the UnixListener and execve's the new binary as 'phux server --resume <fd>' (fds survive exec -> children stay alive, socket stays bound); (3) the new server adopts the fds and rebuilds each pane's Terminal by vt_write-ing its snapshot ('attach to yourself'). Clients blink-reconnect (v1); passing live client sockets for flicker-free swap is v2.

PHASES: v1 = explicit 'phux upgrade' command (the dev-iteration + agent-session win). v2 = auto-detect a newer on-disk binary after cargo install / brew upgrade (the non-dev 'just works' win) + pass client sockets for seamless. Rejected alts: kill+restart (status quo, loses sessions); separate fd-keeper daemon (cleaner crash-isolation but a permanent 2nd process + IPC seam — recorded as v2 hardening); serialize libghostty engine state (not feasible / drifts — replay the VT snapshot instead).

IMPLEMENTATION SLICES (file beads as children): (a) state-blob format (versioned — old<->new server versions straddle it) + ser/de; (b) fd manifest + CLOEXEC clearing + the --resume server mode that adopts inherited PTY masters + listener; (c) the snapshot-all-panes + quiesce (a pane mid-SPAWN / in-flight input must quiesce before snapshot); (d) the re-exec orchestration with a SAFE FALLBACK (validate the new binary via --version / dry --resume probe BEFORE committing; on failure keep serving on the old image — must never strand children); (e) client reconnect-on-swap; (f) 'phux upgrade' command + tests (incl. a child-survives-upgrade integration test: spawn a sleep/echo, upgrade, assert the same child + scrollback survived). SHARP EDGES: scrollback fidelity bounded by the snapshot (styled-scrollback still plain pending phux-q0x7); OSC8/graphics registries deferred same as attach; the state format is a maintained compatibility boundary across releases.
bead.notes:
SCOPED (session 2026-06-10): ADR-0032 = execve re-exec + fd inheritance + VT-snapshot replay (NOT fd-keeper daemon, NOT engine serialization). HARD CONSTRAINT (not in ADR): portable_pty 0.9 exposes MasterPty::as_raw_fd() (read) but NO constructor to rebuild MasterPty/Child from an inherited fd+PID. => Approach A: on resume, bypass PtyOwned with an adopted-PTY backing wrapping File::from_raw_fd(master) + waitpid(pid); keep portable_pty for SPAWN only. ~1100-1500 LOC across ~9 files. Phases: P0 spike (open PTY, clear FD_CLOEXEC, execve, confirm child alive + bytes flow). P1 versioned state blob (phux-server/src/upgrade/{mod,blob}.rs): per-pane {core/wire ids, child_pid, master_raw_fd, dims, cell_px, term, title, cwd, vt_replay_bytes+scrollback_bytes via SnapshotSynthesizer::synthesize_with_scrollback synthesizer.rs:252} + listener fd + next_wire_id counters (registry.rs:51-54); expose raw fd+PID via a new TerminalHandle request channel (mirror ScreenRequest, terminal_actor/mod.rs:148). P2 perform_upgrade: quiesce panes, collect manifest, clear FD_CLOEXEC on masters+UnixListener (rustix), write blob to memfd/temp fd, std::os::unix::process::CommandExt::exec current_exe as 'server --resume <fd>'; PRE-COMMIT validate new binary; GATE the unconditional socket unlink (runtime/mod.rs:392-397). P3 --resume runtime: UnixListener::from_raw_fd, rebuild registry+wire maps, spawn adopted-PTY actors, vt_write snapshot into fresh Terminal (new_with_seed). P4 wire Command::Upgrade (frame.rs:824, #[non_exhaustive] => additive) + handle_command + phux upgrade CLI. P5 client blink-reconnect (ABSENT today: driver.rs:1148 breaks on Disconnected): outer reconnect loop around main_loop, distinguish detach(exit) vs server-vanished(reconnect); ATTACH already resyncs via TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT (reconnect_scenario.rs:105). RISKS: fd adoption (#1), FD_CLOEXEC miss kills a child, exec-in-place (NOT Command::spawn/fork), quiesce ordering, blob version skew, snapshot fidelity (styled scrollback plain-text). Needs manual upgrade DRILL for acceptance. Update ADR-0032 with the fd-adoption gap + cross-platform blob carrier (macOS has no memfd).
DECISION + SPIKE GREEN (session 2026-06-10): going with re-exec v1 (execve + fd inheritance + snapshot replay), framed as STAGE-1 of substrate/projection separation (NOT the end state). Custodian/fd-keeper stays the planned v2 whose real prize is CRASH-durability (surviving our own panics), which re-exec cannot give. CORRECTION to the earlier SCOPED note: the fd-adoption 'real constraint' is NOT ~1100-1500 LOC and does NOT need to bypass PtyOwned. portable_pty's MasterPty/Child are PUBLIC traits and PtyOwned already stores them as trait objects, so the adopted backing is ~250 LOC of two trait impls over (raw_fd, pid) — no portable_pty fork, no library patch, no new direct dep (Downcast is blanket-impl'd; libc already a dep). The 1100-1500 figure is the WHOLE feature; fd-adoption is its CHEAPEST slice, not the blocker. Built it as a standalone, extractable workspace crate: crates/portable-pty-adopt (AdoptedMaster/AdoptedChild; README has TODO(extract) to split to its own repo + crates.io — it fills a genuine upstream gap). TWO proofs pass (clippy -D warnings + fmt clean): (1) tests/adopt_inprocess.rs — adopt a real PTY from (raw_fd,pid) alone and drive read/write/resize/reap; (2) tests/execve_roundtrip.rs — a PTY master fd (CLOEXEC cleared) + its child survive a REAL execve of the parent, and the post-exec image re-adopts via the crate. Core ADR-0032 bet is de-risked. Remaining work is ordinary feature eng (children a-f): the genuinely hard slices are quiesce-ordering and the never-strand-a-child fallback, NOT fd adoption. Next: phux-server depends on portable-pty-adopt in the --resume path (P3).
STAGE-1 LANDED on main 2026-06-11: crate portable-pty-adopt (AdoptedMaster/AdoptedChild) merged via PR #111, commit c2dd990 (rebased, linear). Full just ci green + Linux CI green. fd-adoption bet de-risked; remaining = blob/quiesce/--resume/reconnect/CLI (children a-f). Next consumer hook: phux-server --resume path depends on portable-pty-adopt.
bead.close_reason: Merged as PR #111 (origin/main c2dd990): portable-pty-adopt crate re-adopts PTYs across execve

subject: phux-l5xa
message: Agent-native composited-view introspection: snapshot --rendered (structured cells of the assembled multi-pane frame)

bead.title: Agent-native composited-view introspection: snapshot --rendered (structured cells of the assembled multi-pane frame)
bead.description: phux exposes server grid state per-pane (snapshot --cells, side-effect-free GET_SCREEN) but has NO surface that emits the CLIENT's COMPOSITED view -- the assembled multi-pane frame (layout tiling + dividers + status bar) as the human's glass actually shows it. This gap is why client-composition/render bugs (e.g. phux-3ns5 mirror-resize: server grid was correct 120x39 while the client painted a stranded ~22-row survivor) are invisible to server snapshots and force an EXTERNAL emulator (pyte) to verify the human-facing render. Proposal: a control-plane surface (e.g. phux snapshot --rendered TARGET) that drives the client's existing headless render path (RenderSink -> Vec<u8>, the attach render path already sink-injectable, phux-549) and returns the composited view as STRUCTURED CELLS (grapheme/style/cursor per cell of the assembled frame) -- NOT VT bytes (bytes would need re-parsing by an emulator, reintroducing the symmetric-blindspot/round-trip problem). Net: an agent (or a test, or an assistant debugging) can ask 'what does the assembled screen look like right now' and get cells back, with no external emulator -- closing the loop and making phux self-verifying. Squarely on the 'best terminal for agents' arc (see bd memory agent-terminal-design-signal-...). Open Qs: where composition happens for a headless query (server has grids+layout-in-metadata but layout/tiling is client-side today; either render client-side headless on demand, or teach the server to composite from grids+layout); per-pane focus/cursor semantics in the composited cell stream; relation to the structured GET_GRID idea in the design-signal memory.
bead.notes: SCOPED (session 2026-06-10): Composite CLIENT-SIDE (server has no ratatui/layout/dividers/bar; porting violates ADR-0020). Phase1 phux-core/src/screen.rs: add RenderedFrame{schema_version,cols,rows,cursor:Option<CursorState>,cells:Vec<RenderedCell>} dense row-major, RenderedCell{grapheme:String,style:CellStyle}; reuse CellStyle/CellColor/CursorState. Phase2 (core work) phux-client render.rs: factor render_at_inner cell walk (render.rs:387-447) so a new render_at_cells writes grapheme+style into a caller dense grid (no VT, no re-parse) reading the SAME RowIterator/CellIterator+cursor_viewport/cursor_visible; mirror wide-cell SpacerTail from synthesizer.rs:454-478. compose_full_frame_cells mirrors paint_full_frame (paint.rs:137): compute_layout, fill panes, overlay divider cells (read from dividers.rs compose_buffer ratatui Buffer directly), overlay status bar cells (status_bar.rs:173 Buffer), composited cursor per end_of_frame_cursor policy. Phase3 driver.rs run_headless_rendered alongside run_with_stdout(:450): handshake+snapshot ingest then ONE compose, return RenderedFrame. Phase4 CLI --rendered on Command::Snapshot (commands/mod.rs:190), mutually exclusive with --cells. RISKS: R2 attaches (NOT side-effect-free like GET_SCREEN) - needs --cols/--rows since no TTY; R3 settle timing (wait for all pane mirrors + layout metadata before compose, not a sleep); R4 ratatui Style->CellStyle inverse of to_ratatui_style(status_bar.rs:110), Color lossy for named/indexed. THIS DOUBLES AS THE RENDER-VERIFY HARNESS for x2hm + 4h5a (assert frame.cells[r*cols+c].grapheme/style + cursor).
bead.close_reason: Merged as PR #115: snapshot --rendered composited multi-pane view as dense cells; doubles as the render-verify harness

subject: phux-ign
message: phux-7vx follow-up: per-Terminal TERM as L1 SPAWN field; restore TERM=ghostty when round-trip is proven

bead.title: phux-7vx follow-up: per-Terminal TERM as L1 SPAWN field; restore TERM=ghostty when round-trip is proven
bead.description:
Today's phux-7vx fix swaps the default TERM=ghostty -> TERM=xterm-256color on the server-spawned PTY (pane_actor.rs:default_shell_command). Reason: TERM=ghostty's terminfo advertises 'fullkbd' which newer ncurses interprets as 'kitty keyboard protocol safe', so htop/less/vim/etc. push CSI > N u, libghostty pivots to CSI-u for every keypress, and TUIs that don't speak kitty (like htop) drop quit/F-keys.

Two follow-on tasks live under this:

1. PROVE THE ROUND-TRIP. Validate phux's input/output layer round-trips kitty KIP correctly for at least: htop, less, vim, neovim, fzf, btop. Integration tests using the wave-4 Screen harness (Screen helper at crates/phux-server/tests/common/screen.rs) where possible, manual smoke tests where not. Once this is green, restore TERM=ghostty default and the user gets ghostty's full terminfo capabilities.

2. EXPOSE TERM AS L1 SPAWN CONFIG. Under ADR-0015 the L1 SPAWN message creates a Terminal. The PTY env (TERM, LANG, PHUX_*, etc.) is part of that spawn. Today the server hardcodes TERM in default_shell_command. Move to a per-Terminal env-map field in the SPAWN request so a consumer (TUI or agent) can override it. Default value lives in phux-config (when that crate gets fleshed out). Blocked by L1 cascade for the wire-message shape.

Commit reference: 9734d42 (the current hardcoded swap).
bead.notes: SCOPED (file:line plan, 2026-06): Part 2 (defaults.term phux-config knob + apply_term precedence) ALREADY LANDED — schema.rs:91 term field, default_term() schema.rs:190 = xterm-256color, spawn.rs:131 DEFAULT_TERM, spawn.rs:141 apply_term, attach.rs:487-488 server applies s.term() then wire env wins. Per-spawn TERM is reachable TODAY via SPAWN_TERMINAL.env TERM entry (attach.rs:499-503, precedence doc attach.rs:478-486). REMAINING for phux-ign: first-class optional TERM field on SPAWN (additive). PHASE A wire (DISJOINT from client render): add field::spawn_terminal::TERM=6 (field.rs:239-250); add term: Option<String> to FrameKind::SpawnTerminal (frame.rs:1522-1536, enc frame.rs:1970-1997 write_field id 6 when Some, dec decode.rs:849-885 new arm, default None — _=>{} loop already forward-compat). FrameKind is #[non_exhaustive] (frame.rs:1066) but struct-variant add breaks exhaustive matches: update constructors input_dispatch.rs:715/763 (env stays None, term None), decode.rs:878, tests frame_wire_snapshots.rs:747/761, wire_roundtrip.rs:1570/1936/1955, server tests. PHASE B server: attach.rs handle_spawn_terminal signature (432-439) take term:Option<String>; apply precedence compiled-in -> s.term() (488) -> wire term field -> wire env TERM; thread through runtime/client.rs:717. PHASE C tests: new wire snapshot w/ term field, roundtrip, server spawn test asserting cmd.get_env(TERM). phux-0o8 round-trip proof is ORTHOGONAL and BLOCKED: encoder already pivots correctly (htop_keys.rs proves legacy q=0x71 default + CSI-u after CSI>31u; client decodes inbound CSI u input.rs:55-66; downsample.rs:236-250 gates kbd replies on caps). Gap is NOT in phux — htop doesn't parse CSI-u; real-TUI proof needs htop/nvim/btop/fzf/less pinned in flake.nix (currently absent, only /bin/sh + cat are reproducible fixtures). subject: phux-pxaj
message: [epic] Perfect predictive echo

bead.title: [epic] Perfect predictive echo
bead.description: Predictive (Mosh-class) local echo is experimental and currently being flipped OFF by default because it mispredicts in common real-world cases. Make it robust enough to re-enable by default. Known issues: (1) vi-mode shells (bindkey -v / set -o vi): normal-mode keys h/j/k/l/i/x/dd are COMMANDS not inserts, so every keystroke mispredicts an insert glyph the server then contradicts — the client can't detect vi mode without shell integration; (2) top-left ghost after fast split/focus-change (FIXED phux-cdvr: suspend until authoritative cursor); (3) general fragility under fast layout/screen transitions and alt-screen apps. Direction to evaluate: adaptive auto-back-off (stop predicting after N consecutive reconcile contradictions, resume when typing normalizes); only predict at/after a known prompt boundary; OSC-133 shell-integration signal for prompt + mode; RTT-adaptive enable. Re-enable default only once vi-mode + modal apps are clean. See phux-7ry0, phux-cdvr, phux-9gw.* lineage.
bead.notes:
SCOPED (adaptive-backoff slice): First shippable slice of self-healing predictive echo. GOAL: when the server keeps CONTRADICTING predictions (vi-mode, modal apps, fast transitions), auto-suspend predicting; resume when reconciles confirm cleanly again. Reuses the existing suspend/is_suspended/set_cursor-rearm machinery (phux-cdvr) — no new overlay/render work.

CONTRADICTION-TRACKING DESIGN (all internal, no config knob this slice; thresholds hardcoded const in state.rs, doc as future-tunable like PredictiveConfig note at state.rs:24):
- Add to PredictionState (state.rs:130 struct, after the suspended field): contradiction_streak:u32 (consecutive reconcile passes that contradicted), clean_confirm_streak:u32 (consecutive clean productive passes since auto-suspend), and auto_backed_off:bool to distinguish a BACKOFF suspend from the existing re-anchor suspend so set_cursor unconditional re-arm (state.rs:262) does NOT prematurely clear a backoff.
- New method note_reconcile(&mut self, stats: ReconcileStats) on PredictionState: if stats.contradicted>0 then contradiction_streak+=1, clean_confirm_streak=0, and when streak>=BACKOFF_THRESHOLD (const 3) call internal auto_suspend (sets suspended=true + auto_backed_off=true, drops queue like suspend). If stats.contradicted==0 AND stats.confirmed>0 (clean PRODUCTIVE pass) then clean_confirm_streak+=1, contradiction_streak=0, and when auto_backed_off && clean_confirm_streak>=REARM_THRESHOLD (const 2) clear auto_backed_off + suspended=false. Empty passes (confirmed==0 && contradicted==0) are NEUTRAL — leave streaks alone.
- ReconcileStats already has confirmed/contradicted/pending (reconcile.rs:49) and is computed by reconcile_terminal_output_per_cell. PREFER: reconcile fn calls state.note_reconcile(summary) just before return (reconcile.rs:161) so all callers get backoff free; the contradicted-suffix clear() (reconcile.rs:149) stays untouched. IMPORTANT: streak fields must NOT be reset by clear() or suspend() (clear runs on every contradiction) — keep them out of those resets.
- set_cursor guard (state.rs:262): only clear suspended when !auto_backed_off, so a backoff survives cursor/snapshot syncs and only the clean-confirm path re-arms. Existing re-anchor suspend() (state.rs:201, from reanchor_predict_to_pane) leaves auto_backed_off untouched.

DRIVER HOOK (light): server_frame.rs:528 already discards _stats from reconcile_terminal_output_per_cell. Internal approach = ZERO driver diff (keep the call). Alt: rename _stats to stats and add predict.note_reconcile(stats). Chosen: internal. The wholesale-drain reconcile_terminal_output (reconcile.rs:67) does NOT feed backoff (not a per-cell verdict) — leave it.

CONFIG: no new knob. predictive-echo (phux-config ExperimentalCfg, default false) stays the only surface; backoff is internal and only runs when echo is opted-in. Thresholds = hardcoded consts doc-earmarked for a future PredictiveConfig field. Default stays OFF; this slice makes opt-in self-healing, a later slice flips default once vi-mode clean.

TESTS (mirror reconcile.rs per-cell + state.rs unit module):
- state.rs: 3 contradictions in a row via note_reconcile then is_suspended true & predict_key Skipped; backed-off state survives set_cursor (still suspended); 2 clean productive confirms after backoff then re-armed (predict_key Predicted); empty passes do not move streaks; re-anchor suspend() independent of auto_backed_off.
- reconcile.rs: integration — predict_key inserts then reconcile with contradicting cells 3x via row_reader, assert is_suspended; then confirming cells, eventually re-armed. Reuse row_reader/key_text helpers.

FILES TOUCHED (swarm conflict map): predict/state.rs (struct fields + note_reconcile + auto_suspend + set_cursor guard + tests); predict/reconcile.rs (call note_reconcile before return + integration tests); attach/server_frame.rs (NONE if internal; else 1 line at :528). No config-crate change.

PHASING: P1 state.rs fields + note_reconcile + auto_suspend + set_cursor guard + unit tests. P2 wire into reconcile_terminal_output_per_cell + integration tests. P3 verify zero/one-line driver hook at server_frame.rs:528. P4 docs: predict/mod.rs backoff paragraph + consts as future-tunable. OUT OF SCOPE: OSC-133, RTT-adaptive, flipping default ON.

subject: phux-7143
message: copy-mode: engine-driven word/line/all/semantic-output select on client

bead.title: copy-mode: engine-driven word/line/all/semantic-output select on client
bead.description: Wire copy-mode's word/line/all and semantic-prompt select-output to the client's own libghostty engine via select_word/select_line/select_all/select_output (SelectLineOptions::with_semantic_prompt_boundary), on slot.terminal. Client-local per ADR-0030. Builds on the client-local copy bead. Parent: phux-abi.
bead.notes:
SCOPED (read-only investigation, 2026-06-11):

ARCHITECTURE / BOUNDARY: Selection is a client-local projection (ADR-0030). The overlay (render/overlay/copy_mode.rs) holds NO terminal ref — only pane-local row/col + SelectionMode, and emits a coordinate-only CopyRequest {start/end row/col, rectangle}. Engine resolution lives entirely in attach/copy.rs (copy_to_host_clipboard -> extract_selection_text -> Terminal::grid_ref + Selection::new + format_selection_alloc), invoked from the input_dispatch.rs copy-mode arm (OverlayOutcome::Copy at line ~184-194) against slot.terminal. render/ never imports libghostty_vt (CI boundary guard) — keep it that way.

NOT BLOCKED ON phux-97w. 97w's dangling-pointer bug is ONLY in the Formatter{selection:Some(_)} path (Formatter::new_inner). The one-shot format_selection_alloc + FormatOptions::with_selection path is SOUND at the current pin 6d49dfd and is already what copy.rs uses (confirmed in phux-server/src/search.rs lines 39-60). select_word/select_line/select_all/select_output ALL have safe Rust wrappers on Terminal at this pin (selection.rs:201-336): select_all(), select_line(SelectLineOptions::new(ref).with_semantic_prompt_boundary(true)), select_output(grid_ref), select_word(SelectWordOptions::new(ref)). Selection exposes start()/end()/is_rectangle(). All option types public under libghostty_vt::selection::*. EVERYTHING is doable NOW.

PLAN (phased, client-only, NO wire/protocol change):
P1 verbs that DON'T need cursor grid_ref: extend SelectionMode -> add a SelectionGrab enum carried on CopyRequest (or a new OverlayCommand::Copy variant param). 'A'=select-all maps to Terminal::select_all(); engine derives Selection directly, format via existing format_selection_alloc. Add key 'A' in copy_mode.rs handle_key.
P2 verbs that NEED the cursor grid_ref (resolved in copy.rs, not overlay): 'w'=word -> select_word(SelectWordOptions::new(grid_ref(Point::Viewport{cursor_col,cursor_row}))); 'V'=line -> select_line(SelectLineOptions::new(ref)); semantic 'V' variant -> .with_semantic_prompt_boundary(true). For these the overlay must pass its cursor (row,col) through CopyRequest so copy.rs can build grid_ref(Point::Viewport) and call the derive helper instead of building a linear Selection from two corners.
P3 select-semantic-output: ']' or similar -> select_output(grid_ref at cursor). Requires OSC-133 zones in the pane's mirror; degrade to None (no-op, best-effort) when absent — matches existing blank-span no-op contract.

KEY DESIGN CHOICE: add an enum (e.g. SelectionGrab { Rect, Word, Line, LineSemantic, All, Output }) to CopyRequest replacing/augmenting the bool . copy.rs::extract_selection_text matches on it: Rect/Char keep the two-corner Selection::new path; the rest call the derive helpers with a grid_ref at (cursor_row,cursor_col) and format the returned Selection. Overlay must carry cursor coords in CopyRequest (it already has cursor_row/col).

FILES TOUCHED (stays within render/overlay/ + input_dispatch copy-mode arm + attach/copy.rs — low swarm-conflict surface):
- crates/phux-client/src/render/overlay/copy_mode.rs (add key bindings w/V/A/], extend copy_request())
- crates/phux-client/src/render/overlay/mod.rs (extend CopyRequest with grab enum + cursor coords)
- crates/phux-client/src/attach/copy.rs (match grab enum -> derive helper vs two-corner Selection)
- crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs (optional: status strip text per grab mode)
- input_dispatch.rs copy-mode arm unchanged structurally (already routes OverlayOutcome::Copy)

TEST STRATEGY: unit tests in copy.rs (extract via select_word/line/all/output using fresh Terminal + vt_write fixtures, incl. OSC-133 \x1b]133;A/B/C\x07 fixtures for semantic line + select_output — see synthesizer.rs:1680 for the fixture pattern); key-binding tests in copy_mode.rs (w/V/A produce CopyRequest with correct grab). No protocol/wire tests needed (client-local). cargo nextest run -p phux-client; clippy -D warnings.
CORRECTION (shell ate one line above): the SelectionGrab enum AUGMENTS rather than replaces the existing rectangle bool — Rect path keeps using the two-corner Selection::new(start,end,true); the new grab variants (Word/Line/LineSemantic/All/Output) instead call the derive helpers with a grid_ref at the overlay cursor and format the RETURNED Selection. Net: copy.rs::extract_selection_text branches on grab; everything else unchanged.
bead.close_reason: Merged as PR #119: engine-driven word/line/all/output copy-mode select via libghostty one-shot wrappers (client-local, ADR-0030)

subject: phux-7ubw
message: Client mirroring/letterbox: render a referenced Terminal letterboxed when view size differs

bead.title: Client mirroring/letterbox: render a referenced Terminal letterboxed when view size differs
bead.description:
ADR-0027 (decision points 1-2). When multiple views reference one Terminal and a view's slot is larger than the Terminal's authoritative (cols, rows), render the Terminal mirrored and LETTERBOXED in that view rather than reflowing the shared grid (a Terminal has one PTY winsize / one grid width). When the chosen size is larger than a view, the view clamps. Honest, documented behavior per ADR-0027, matching tmux.

DEPENDS ON: phux-xjgs (window-size config key) — the letterbox path needs the resolved policy to know WHICH size is authoritative before it can render the others letterboxed/clamped. Also relates to phux-nk07 (multi-client viewport ownership) for the server-side geometry pick.

v1 mirrors share scroll (per-view scroll is deferred in ADR-0027).

LANE NOTE: client letterbox/mirroring rendering code — kept out of the window-size config PR by design.

Source: ADR/0027-terminal-references-and-l3-links.md, decision points 1, 2, 3 (deferred scroll).
bead.notes:
SCOPE ASSESSMENT (2026-06-07, after landing the nk07/xjgs server policy in PR #76). Two findings make this a dedicated, real-TUI-verified PR rather than a quick follow-up:

1. MIRRORING IS STRUCTURALLY UNBUILT. The headline premise ('multiple views reference one Terminal') has no consumer: crates/phux-client-core/src/multi_pane/layout.rs pane_rects() returns HashMap<TerminalId, Rect> — one slot per Terminal. The layout tree's leaves map uniquely to TerminalIds; the same Terminal cannot occupy two slots. Mirroring needs a layout-model change (allow a Terminal in N leaves) first.

2. THE REACHABLE SLICE (single-view letterbox when the policy-chosen grid < the client's slot, now possible post-nk07) requires UNWINDING the client-drives-geometry model. Today crates/phux-client/src/attach/paint.rs paint_focused_pane() calls safe_resize(slot.terminal, rect.w, rect.h) — it forces the LOCAL libghostty mirror to the slot size and renders at the rect origin, and the reflow pipeline (attach/reflow.rs) emits RESIZE to drive the PTY to that slot (rasterize.rs:64-67: 'the rect a pane is painted into always equals the rect pane_rects tells the server to size the PTY to'). To letterbox, the client must instead size its mirror to the SERVER's authoritative grid (the snapshot cols/rows the nk07 policy picked), compute a centered offset within the rect, render_at that offset, and clear the margins — i.e. defer to the server's geometry instead of owning it. That touches paint + reflow + the RESIZE-emission loop together and must be verified in a real multi-client TUI (two clients of different sizes on one session), which a headless test can't fully cover.

RECOMMEND: split into (a) single-view letterbox under the nk07 policy [reachable now, the valuable 80%], and (b) true mirroring [needs the layout-model multi-leaf change first]. (a) is the next bead to take; (b) waits on a layout-model bead. The server half it depended on (phux-xjgs/nk07) is shipped.
SCOPED (2026-06-11, render plan): Single-view letterbox is render-only, NO wire/protocol/driver-hub changes. The mirror is ALREADY server-authoritative (server_frame.rs:271-278 is the ONE resize site; paint_focused_pane/paint_full_frame already DO NOT resize the mirror to the rect — phux-wurs landed that). Today render_at_inner (render.rs:325-481) CLIPS via rows_total=min(mirror,clip), cols_total=min(mirror,clip) (render.rs:385-386) at origin (rect.x,rect.y). Letterbox = change clip->center: when mirror dims < rect, compute centered sub-rect (pad_x=(rect.w-mirror_cols)/2, pad_y=(rect.h-mirror_rows)/2), shift CUP origin by the pad, and paint blank-fill bars in the 4 margins; when mirror > rect keep today's clip (clamp). PLAN: (P1) add a pure helper letterbox_rect(rect, mirror_cols, mirror_rows)->(inner_origin, inner_clip, margins) — unit-testable, no I/O. (P2) thread an effective_origin/effective_clip + margin-fill into render_at_inner OR add render_at_letterboxed wrapper that emits margin bars then calls the existing core at the centered origin; keep render_at(min-clip) as the clamp path. (P3) call it from paint.rs paint_focused_pane (render.rs:80-86) and paint_full_frame non-focused loop (paint.rs:178-184) using slot.terminal.cols()/rows() vs rect.w/h. (P4) margin fill: emit ' ' runs (or theme bg) for the pad rows/cols, each a CUP+blank-run; reset SGR. FILES: render.rs (+ helper +letterbox path), paint.rs (call sites, pass mirror dims). STAYS WITHIN render.rs+paint.rs — does NOT touch driver hub, reflow.rs, server_frame.rs, or layout/rasterize. TESTS (headless, in render.rs #[cfg(test)]): mirror smaller than rect -> centered (CUP offset by pad), correct N margin rows/cols blanked; mirror == rect -> no padding (byte-identical to today); mirror larger than rect -> clamps (existing clip tests stay green); odd-diff centering (floor split, extra pad on bottom/right); cursor last_cursor includes pad offset. DEPENDS-ON note unchanged: true multi-leaf mirroring (same Terminal in N slots) still needs the layout-model change (client-core multi_pane/layout.rs pane_rects HashMap<TerminalId,Rect> is 1 slot/Terminal) — OUT OF SCOPE for this render-only cut; this delivers the reachable single-view letterbox under the nk07/xjgs policy.
bead.close_reason: Merged as PR #120: single-view letterbox renders an undersized mirror centered with blank margins; mirror>=rect clamps as before. Multi-leaf mirroring (one Terminal in N slots) tracked separately (needs layout-model change).

subject: phux-4h5a
message: Warp-style session/window sidebar + pluggable chrome regions

bead.title: Warp-style session/window sidebar + pluggable chrome regions
bead.description: Toggleable vertical sidebar (default left, ~20 cols) listing windows as tabs, each titled from its OSC title (now available via window_infos, #95) with the focused tab highlighted; click/select to focus. Delivers the 'chrome modular and pluggable' ask via a ChromeRegion reservation abstraction (sidebar = just another region alongside status bar + dividers). Default-off to keep the main path snapshot-stable.
bead.design:
PHASED (each independently shippable):

P1 Chrome-region reservation + viewport offset. Today reservation is 1-D: pane_viewport() only shrinks height for the status bar; pane rects tile from (0,0). compute_layout/pane_rects walk_layout from Rect{0,0,w,h} — passing a non-zero-origin Rect offsets all leaf rects AND divider cells automatically (no separate offset pass). Risk = the ~8 viewport->pane-area sites that must all consume the same reservation or panes/dividers/reflow(TERMINAL_RESIZE)/mouse/predict disagree: paint.rs pane_viewport+2 compute_layout calls+single-pane fallback; server_frame.rs snapshot+output paint origins+fallbacks; driver.rs SIGWINCH reflow prev/new dims; reflow.rs pane_rects; mouse.rs route_mouse_event+clamp; actions.rs min-cell gate. Predict follows for free (it reads slot.renderer.last_origin from the rect). rasterize() takes (cols,rows) for edge-junction math — must take the content rect so sidebar-boundary junctions are right. Introduce ChromeRegion{reserve(Edge,thickness); paint(rect,ctx)} + ChromeLayout folding regions off the outer rect to a residual content Rect; re-express pane_viewport in terms of it (status-bar-only residual is byte-identical => snapshots stable).

P2 Sidebar painter (render/chrome/sidebar.rs impl ChromeRegion): one row per window 'N title', active row themed (new slots sidebar_active/inactive/border, default to accent/dim/border). Feed from window_infos(&workspace,&panes) (already title-aware). Cache like StatusBarPainter (unchanged input => zero-byte repaint). Reserve Edge::Left when [sidebar].enabled.

P3 already partly done: titles come from window_infos (#95, phux-efj7).

P4 Mouse+keys: route_mouse_event sidebar pre-check (x<width => window index => switch_window); new actions toggle-sidebar (driver-held bool, layout_mutated=true to reflow) + focus-window(index), added to ACTION_NAMES + run_action + action_registry REGISTRY (drift test enforces).

P5 Config: [sidebar] enabled(false)/width(20)/position(Left|Right) in schema.rs; theme slots.

No wire/L3/ADR change (client-only chrome; titles from the client's own libghostty mirror; sidebar visibility is per-client render state like focus, ADR-0019 dec 6). Default-off keeps every existing snapshot/test green; the enabled path gets its own tests. Full design + file:line anchors captured in session 2026-06-10.
bead.notes: INTEGRATION (2/2) turnkey site list — thread a content-rect reservation (sidebar inset by width+position; full pane area when disabled, so default-off == compute_layout, snapshot-stable). Replace compute_layout(ls, pane_dims) with compute_layout_in(ls, content_rect, viewport_dims) and pane_rects with pane_rects_in at: paint.rs:62 (paint_focused_pane), paint.rs:159 (paint_full_frame); server_frame.rs:253,364,469,557,585; driver.rs:1166,1417,1495 (SIGWINCH reflow + repaint); reflow.rs:112 (compute_reflow); actions.rs:358 (min-cell gate). Also: route_mouse_event (mouse.rs) needs the content offset + a sidebar hit-zone; driver builds SidebarPainter from cfg.sidebar + paints it into the reserved rect after panes; add toggle-sidebar + focus-window actions (ACTION_NAMES + run_action + action_registry REGISTRY drift test). Foundation merged: #98 (compute_layout_in/pane_rects_in), #99 (SidebarCfg + SidebarPainter). Verify the resize handshake live (memory: see-rendering-via-emulator) since reflow timing is involved.
bead.close_reason: Merged as PR #124: window sidebar 2/2 — chrome-region reservation (content_rect) threaded through paint/server-frame/reflow/resize/min-cell/rendered-compositor; SidebarPainter strip + toggle-sidebar action; default-off byte-identical; enabled path verified via snapshot --rendered harness. Mouse click-to-focus + focus-window-by-index deferred (marked in-code).

subject: phux-shz9
message: [epic] Swarm batch — parallel backlog map-reduce

bead.title: [epic] Swarm batch — parallel backlog map-reduce
bead.description:
ENTRY POINT for a fresh-session swarm. Goal: map-reduce the phux backlog across parallel agents.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINT (learned 2026-06-10/11): client changes funnel through driver.rs / server_frame.rs / input_dispatch.rs / paint.rs / render.rs. Agents editing these in parallel collide in the REDUCE (merge) phase. So: parallelize ACROSS disjoint lanes; SERIALIZE within the client lane (rebase + just ci between each). origin/main moves under you (multiple concurrent agents in this workspace — main checkout is on other branches; NEVER touch it, always use your own worktree off origin/main).

WORKFLOW per item: worktree off origin/main -> implement -> nix develop --command just ci GREEN -> clippy+fmt preflight (backtick doc identifiers; map_or not map().unwrap_or()) -> push (gh auth switch --user phall1 first) -> PR -> watch CI -> rebase onto moving main if needed + re-run just ci -> merge -> close bead. Verify TUI render changes via emulator where possible (snapshot --cells); assembled-frame render is the one thing CI can't prove.

LANE A — Client render/UX (SERIALIZE; shared hub). Order matters:
  A1 phux-jow6 mouse-while-zoomed (quick) [turnkey: bead has fix]
  A2 phux-l5xa snapshot --rendered HARNESS [turnkey] -- DO EARLY: unblocks verified render for A3+
  A3 phux-4h5a sidebar 2/2 [turnkey: foundation merged #98/#99, integration plan on bead] -- verify with A2
  A4 phux-7143 copy-mode word/line/semantic select [scoped by agent]
  A5 phux-7ubw letterbox/mirror [scoped by agent]

LANE B — Server/transport (PARALLEL to A; disjoint files):
  B0 phux-fak5 graceful upgrade -- IN PROGRESS by another agent (feat/portable-pty-adopt); COORDINATE, do not duplicate
  B1 phux-ign + phux-0o8 per-Terminal TERM=ghostty SPAWN field + KIP round-trip [scoped by agent] -- protocol+server, disjoint
  B2 phux-84yt QUIC transport (P0) -- LARGE, needs its own sub-decomposition before swarming

LANE C — Predict (PARALLEL; predict/ module + light server_frame hook):
  C1 phux-pxaj adaptive auto-back-off slice [scoped by agent]

DEFERRED/BLOCKED: phux-97w (upstream libghostty pin) blocks phux-3sy scrollback search; epics phux-klxy/phux-0wmf/phux-yx6y/phux-nol/phux-51n6 need decomposition first.
EXCLUDED (not phux): gc/mol beads phux-887dj/9fq0j/jiesq/u02uj/zklq4.

REDUCE: merge in dep order (A1->A2->A3; B/C parallel); each rebases onto current origin/main + re-verifies just ci before merge. Keep PRs small. Done beads this lineage: phux-7ry0, phux-v9zw, phux-efj7, phux-9ydy, phux-5pyx, phux-ac14, phux-jy4t, phux-x2hm (all merged).
bead.notes:
CORRECTION (scoping results): Lane B1 is phux-ign ONLY (per-spawn TERM as additive SPAWN frame field 6 + server apply_term 4th-tier; protocol+server, disjoint from client render — turnkey). phux-0o8 is NOT swarm-able: BLOCKED on flake.nix pinning a KIP-aware TUI (htop/nvim/btop) for a reproducible round-trip test; it's a fixture+manual-smoke gate before flipping default_term to ghostty, not an implementation. Move phux-0o8 to deferred. LANE REFINEMENT from scoping: phux-7ubw letterbox = render.rs+paint.rs ONLY (no driver/server_frame); phux-pxaj backoff = predict/state.rs+reconcile.rs ONLY (no hub). So within Lane A the true file-collision groups are: {jow6: input_dispatch} {l5xa+7ubw: render.rs/paint.rs} {4h5a: driver+server_frame+paint+mouse+layout} {7143: render/overlay+input_dispatch copy-mode arm}. pxaj(predict) and ign(protocol/server) are fully parallel to all of Lane A.
FINAL — all 4 scopes landed; every picked item turnkey (plans on each bead). Refined collision groups (run any disjoint pair in parallel):
  G1 {phux-jow6}        input_dispatch (mouse arm)
  G2 {phux-l5xa,7ubw}   render.rs + paint.rs (+core/screen for l5xa)
  G3 {phux-4h5a}        driver+server_frame+paint+mouse+layout (heaviest)
  G4 {phux-7143}        render/overlay/copy_mode + overlay/mod + attach/copy.rs  (DISJOINT from G1-G3!)
  G5 {phux-pxaj}        predict/state + reconcile (no hub)
  G6 {phux-ign}         protocol wire + server spawn (no client render)
=> up to 6 agents can run concurrently if each takes a different group; the only true serialize is WITHIN a group. Suggested first wave: jow6(G1)+l5xa(G2)+7143(G4)+pxaj(G5)+ign(G6) in parallel; then 4h5a(G3) after l5xa lands (wants the snapshot --rendered harness), 7ubw(G2) after l5xa.
UNBLOCKED: phux-3sy scrollback search is NOT blocked on 97w after all — the one-shot select_* API (select_word/line/all/output, search.rs:39-60) is sound; only Formatter{selection:Some} is buggy. phux-3sy joins as G4-adjacent (server/core search + libghostty wrappers). phux-7143 also closes the libghostty side of 3sy/97w.
phux-0o8 stays DEFERRED (flake.nix KIP-TUI fixture gap, manual gate).
bead.close_reason: Swarm batch COMPLETE. All swarm-able work-list items landed on main + beads closed: phux-jow6 (#113 mouse-while-zoomed), phux-l5xa (#115 snapshot --rendered harness), phux-ign (#116 per-spawn TERM L1 field), phux-pxaj slice (#117 adaptive predict back-off; parent epic stays open), phux-7143 (#119 engine copy-mode select), phux-7ubw (#120 single-view letterbox), phux-4h5a (#124 window sidebar 2/2), phux-3sy (search shipped earlier + selection wrappers via #119; extraction deferred to upstream-blocked phux-97w). phux-fak5 was already merged (#111) and closed. Correctly DEFERRED/EXCLUDED per the epic plan: phux-84yt (QUIC P0, needs sub-decomposition), phux-0o8 (flake.nix KIP-TUI fixture gate), phux-97w (upstream libghostty Formatter pin), gc/mol beads, and undecomposed epics (klxy/0wmf/yx6y/nol/51n6). Lane serialization (client render hub) held; each PR rebased onto moving main + just-ci-green before merge.

subject: phux-fak5
message: Graceful server upgrade: sessions survive a binary update (ADR-0032)

bead.title: Graceful server upgrade: sessions survive a binary update (ADR-0032)
bead.description:
Per ADR-0032 (Proposed). Today a binary upgrade requires 'phux kill' -> restart, which closes every PTY master and SIGHUPs the children (shells, editors, the Claude agent session) — the maintainer hit this directly (installing a fix killed the session hosting the work). tmux has the same hole; phux can close it because it ALREADY emits a replayable per-pane snapshot (vt_replay_bytes + scrollback_bytes via SnapshotSynthesizer, ADR-0013).

APPROACH (ADR-0032): graceful in-place upgrade via execve re-exec + fd inheritance + VT snapshot replay. On 'phux upgrade' the running server (1) snapshots every pane + serializes the session/window/pane tree + an fd->pane manifest into an inherited state fd; (2) clears FD_CLOEXEC on the PTY masters + the UnixListener and execve's the new binary as 'phux server --resume <fd>' (fds survive exec -> children stay alive, socket stays bound); (3) the new server adopts the fds and rebuilds each pane's Terminal by vt_write-ing its snapshot ('attach to yourself'). Clients blink-reconnect (v1); passing live client sockets for flicker-free swap is v2.

PHASES: v1 = explicit 'phux upgrade' command (the dev-iteration + agent-session win). v2 = auto-detect a newer on-disk binary after cargo install / brew upgrade (the non-dev 'just works' win) + pass client sockets for seamless. Rejected alts: kill+restart (status quo, loses sessions); separate fd-keeper daemon (cleaner crash-isolation but a permanent 2nd process + IPC seam — recorded as v2 hardening); serialize libghostty engine state (not feasible / drifts — replay the VT snapshot instead).

IMPLEMENTATION SLICES (file beads as children): (a) state-blob format (versioned — old<->new server versions straddle it) + ser/de; (b) fd manifest + CLOEXEC clearing + the --resume server mode that adopts inherited PTY masters + listener; (c) the snapshot-all-panes + quiesce (a pane mid-SPAWN / in-flight input must quiesce before snapshot); (d) the re-exec orchestration with a SAFE FALLBACK (validate the new binary via --version / dry --resume probe BEFORE committing; on failure keep serving on the old image — must never strand children); (e) client reconnect-on-swap; (f) 'phux upgrade' command + tests (incl. a child-survives-upgrade integration test: spawn a sleep/echo, upgrade, assert the same child + scrollback survived). SHARP EDGES: scrollback fidelity bounded by the snapshot (styled-scrollback still plain pending phux-q0x7); OSC8/graphics registries deferred same as attach; the state format is a maintained compatibility boundary across releases.
bead.notes:
SCOPED (session 2026-06-10): ADR-0032 = execve re-exec + fd inheritance + VT-snapshot replay (NOT fd-keeper daemon, NOT engine serialization). HARD CONSTRAINT (not in ADR): portable_pty 0.9 exposes MasterPty::as_raw_fd() (read) but NO constructor to rebuild MasterPty/Child from an inherited fd+PID. => Approach A: on resume, bypass PtyOwned with an adopted-PTY backing wrapping File::from_raw_fd(master) + waitpid(pid); keep portable_pty for SPAWN only. ~1100-1500 LOC across ~9 files. Phases: P0 spike (open PTY, clear FD_CLOEXEC, execve, confirm child alive + bytes flow). P1 versioned state blob (phux-server/src/upgrade/{mod,blob}.rs): per-pane {core/wire ids, child_pid, master_raw_fd, dims, cell_px, term, title, cwd, vt_replay_bytes+scrollback_bytes via SnapshotSynthesizer::synthesize_with_scrollback synthesizer.rs:252} + listener fd + next_wire_id counters (registry.rs:51-54); expose raw fd+PID via a new TerminalHandle request channel (mirror ScreenRequest, terminal_actor/mod.rs:148). P2 perform_upgrade: quiesce panes, collect manifest, clear FD_CLOEXEC on masters+UnixListener (rustix), write blob to memfd/temp fd, std::os::unix::process::CommandExt::exec current_exe as 'server --resume <fd>'; PRE-COMMIT validate new binary; GATE the unconditional socket unlink (runtime/mod.rs:392-397). P3 --resume runtime: UnixListener::from_raw_fd, rebuild registry+wire maps, spawn adopted-PTY actors, vt_write snapshot into fresh Terminal (new_with_seed). P4 wire Command::Upgrade (frame.rs:824, #[non_exhaustive] => additive) + handle_command + phux upgrade CLI. P5 client blink-reconnect (ABSENT today: driver.rs:1148 breaks on Disconnected): outer reconnect loop around main_loop, distinguish detach(exit) vs server-vanished(reconnect); ATTACH already resyncs via TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT (reconnect_scenario.rs:105). RISKS: fd adoption (#1), FD_CLOEXEC miss kills a child, exec-in-place (NOT Command::spawn/fork), quiesce ordering, blob version skew, snapshot fidelity (styled scrollback plain-text). Needs manual upgrade DRILL for acceptance. Update ADR-0032 with the fd-adoption gap + cross-platform blob carrier (macOS has no memfd).
DECISION + SPIKE GREEN (session 2026-06-10): going with re-exec v1 (execve + fd inheritance + snapshot replay), framed as STAGE-1 of substrate/projection separation (NOT the end state). Custodian/fd-keeper stays the planned v2 whose real prize is CRASH-durability (surviving our own panics), which re-exec cannot give. CORRECTION to the earlier SCOPED note: the fd-adoption 'real constraint' is NOT ~1100-1500 LOC and does NOT need to bypass PtyOwned. portable_pty's MasterPty/Child are PUBLIC traits and PtyOwned already stores them as trait objects, so the adopted backing is ~250 LOC of two trait impls over (raw_fd, pid) — no portable_pty fork, no library patch, no new direct dep (Downcast is blanket-impl'd; libc already a dep). The 1100-1500 figure is the WHOLE feature; fd-adoption is its CHEAPEST slice, not the blocker. Built it as a standalone, extractable workspace crate: crates/portable-pty-adopt (AdoptedMaster/AdoptedChild; README has TODO(extract) to split to its own repo + crates.io — it fills a genuine upstream gap). TWO proofs pass (clippy -D warnings + fmt clean): (1) tests/adopt_inprocess.rs — adopt a real PTY from (raw_fd,pid) alone and drive read/write/resize/reap; (2) tests/execve_roundtrip.rs — a PTY master fd (CLOEXEC cleared) + its child survive a REAL execve of the parent, and the post-exec image re-adopts via the crate. Core ADR-0032 bet is de-risked. Remaining work is ordinary feature eng (children a-f): the genuinely hard slices are quiesce-ordering and the never-strand-a-child fallback, NOT fd adoption. Next: phux-server depends on portable-pty-adopt in the --resume path (P3).
STAGE-1 LANDED on main 2026-06-11: crate portable-pty-adopt (AdoptedMaster/AdoptedChild) merged via PR #111, commit c2dd990 (rebased, linear). Full just ci green + Linux CI green. fd-adoption bet de-risked; remaining = blob/quiesce/--resume/reconnect/CLI (children a-f). Next consumer hook: phux-server --resume path depends on portable-pty-adopt.
SHIPPED v1 (2026-06-11). Graceful upgrade complete across 9 PRs merged to main: #111 portable-pty-adopt crate (fd re-adoption, execve-survival proof) | #112 versioned StateBlob format | #114 per-pane upgrade-handle actor request | #118 build_upgrade_blob producer | #121 new_with_adopted_pty (resumed actor) | #122 rebuild_from_blob consumer | #123 server --resume mode (adopt listener + rebuild) | #125 UPGRADE wire command (tag 0x0e, spec 0.5.0-draft.4) | #126 perform_upgrade re-exec orchestration + handle_upgrade + phux upgrade CLI + child-survives acceptance drill (PASSES) | #127 client blink-reconnect | #128 ADR-0032 -> Accepted. KEY FINDING: the 'real constraint' (portable_pty can't re-adopt) was shallow — MasterPty/Child are PUBLIC traits, so ~250 LOC of impls (no fork), extracted as portable-pty-adopt crate (TODO: split to own repo + crates.io). Blob carrier = anonymous tempfile (not memfd; macOS has none). Acceptance: server PID unchanged across upgrade (in-place execve), pane child alive, scrollback survives. REMAINING (v2, not blocking): flicker-free client-socket handoff; crash-surviving fd-keeper daemon; styled-scrollback fidelity (phux-q0x7); auto-detect newer on-disk binary. Suggest closing this bead; open v2 follow-ups separately.
bead.close_reason: Merged as PR #111 (origin/main c2dd990): portable-pty-adopt crate re-adopts PTYs across execve

subject: phux-2sl6
message: Agent-asked over the wire: detect ASKED server-side and emit to consumers

bead.title: Agent-asked over the wire: detect ASKED server-side and emit to consumers
bead.description: Umbrella epic for the phux half of the coding-agent notification initiative. Today the server emits AgentEvent frames (0xB3) for bell/title/dirty/idle/pane lifecycle (crates/phux-server/src/terminal_actor/mod.rs:1000-1117; crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/frame.rs:1005-1064) but there is NO wire source for "an agent asked a human a question". TERMINAL_EVENT (0xB1) + TerminalEventBody::USER_NOTIFICATION (docs/spec/L1.md:71,183-194) and RUN_HOOK (L1.md:436) are spec-only, unimplemented. This epic decides the mechanism, decides how the server detects the ASKED state (clean-room agent-lifecycle capture, NOT a copy of AGPL herdr), implements emission, and bumps the protocol minor (currently 0.5.0 per crates/phux-protocol/src/lib.rs:83-85). phux-mobile and clients/phux-web are the downstream consumers; the wire is owned here.
bead.design: Two design decisions are spiked first (emission mechanism; detection source), each lands an ADR, then implementation. Additive TLV encoding keeps the change forward-compatible (frame.rs Unknown variant, skip-by-length).
bead.acceptance_criteria: All child beads closed: a phux server build emits a structured agent-asked event on the SUBSCRIBE_EVENTS stream when an agent enters ASKED, the protocol minor is bumped, the spec/CHANGELOG documents the new event, and a wire integration test asserts a subscriber receives it.
bead.notes: Linchpin of the whole initiative. The closed phux-mobile-5g5 close note ("Wire-level support requires a phux server change to emit the ASKED event") points straight here.

subject: phux-2sl6.1
message: Decide the agent-asked wire mechanism (new AgentEvent vs TERMINAL_EVENT vs RUN_HOOK)

bead.title: Decide the agent-asked wire mechanism (new AgentEvent vs TERMINAL_EVENT vs RUN_HOOK)
bead.description: The emission mechanism is genuinely unsettled (a real protocol decision, not readable from source). Candidates: (a) a new additive AgentEvent variant Asked{ id, question, suggestions?, ... } on the existing 0xB3 stream -- cheapest, forward-compatible; (b) implement the spec-only TERMINAL_EVENT 0xB1 frame + TerminalEventBody::USER_NOTIFICATION (OSC 9 / OSC 777) at docs/spec/L1.md:183-194; (c) implement the spec-only RUN_HOOK command (L1.md:436, tag 0x06 reserved in frame.rs:403-410) as an agent-hook dispatch point. The downstream Swift consumer already expects agentAsked(terminalID, AgentQuestion{ id, text, suggestions[], waitingSeconds }) (phux-mobile Packages/PhuxKit/Sources/PhuxProtocol/Model.swift:22-45) -- the chosen payload must carry that shape. Resolve whether OSC 9/777 are currently parsed-and-dropped (L1.md:228 "server does not forward every OSC type") or forwardable.
bead.design: Read docs/spec/proto.md:203-213 (versioning), L1.md sec 3.3 and 5.1. Prefer the additive AgentEvent variant unless USER_NOTIFICATION/OSC reuse is clearly superior.
bead.acceptance_criteria: An ADR is written and merged in phux choosing exactly one mechanism with rationale, defining the wire payload (fields + tags) and the minor-version bump, AND this spike FILES the downstream implementation beads (the protocol-type bead and, if 0xB1/RUN_HOOK is chosen, the frame-implementation bead) under this epic with the decided shape.
bead.notes: Bead-that-creates-beads for the wire shape. Do not pre-write the impl beads here; emit them once the mechanism is chosen.

subject: phux-2sl6.2
message: Determine how the server detects an agent has entered ASKED

bead.title: Determine how the server detects an agent has entered ASKED
bead.description: Independent of the wire mechanism, the server must DECIDE that an agent is asking. Three clean-room-reusable patterns from herdr (AGPL-3.0 -- PATTERN ONLY, NO source copy): (a) opt-in agent lifecycle hooks reporting state over a channel (authoritative when live), (b) per-agent screen-scrape manifests (evidence gates over OSC title + bottom grid lines), (c) OSC title/progress. phux already owns the PTYs server-side via libghostty, so scrape is feasible. This needs a build/run to confirm what OSC sequences real agents (Claude Code, Codex, pi) actually emit when blocking on input, and which detection layer phux wants to own first. Requires an ADR because it closes off a design space (hook contract vs manifest authority vs both).
bead.design: Clean-room: reuse only the design pattern (hook-authority-wins, scrape-fallback, suppress-on-exit). Author phux own manifests/contract from scratch.
bead.acceptance_criteria: An ADR is written and merged in phux choosing the detection source(s) and (if hooks) the namespaced hook/OSC contract agents implement, with an explicit clean-room note that no AGPL herdr source/manifests/hook scripts are copied. The spike empirically records what at least Claude Code and one other agent emit on block, AND FILES the implementation bead(s) for the chosen detection module under this epic.
bead.notes: AGPL boundary: herdr src/detect/manifests/*.toml and src/integration/assets/* are off-limits to copy. Pattern reuse is fine.

message: Emit the agent-asked event on SUBSCRIBE_EVENTS when a pane enters ASKED

bead.title: Emit the agent-asked event on SUBSCRIBE_EVENTS when a pane enters ASKED
bead.description: Wire the detection module to broadcast the new wire type through the existing event path: emit_event / broadcast_agent_event (crates/phux-server/src/terminal_actor/mod.rs:1086-1117) so both attached and unattached SUBSCRIBE_EVENTS (0x41) subscribers receive it, exactly as bell/title/idle do today (tests/agent_events.rs). This is the linchpin deliverable: once merged, phux-mobile and phux-web can consume real agent-asked events. Add a wire integration test mirroring tests/agent_events.rs asserting a subscriber receives the agent-asked event.
bead.design: Coalesce like Dirty/Idle so repeated frames during a single ask do not re-fire.
bead.acceptance_criteria: A server build emits the agent-asked event to subscribers on transition to ASKED; wire integration test green for both attached and unattached subscribers; idempotent on redraw (fires once per genuine new ask, not per output burst).
bead.notes: Unblocks the phux-mobile bridge-consume and pin-bump beads (cross-tracker).

bead.description: Wire the detection module to broadcast the new wire type through the existing event path: emit_event / broadcast_agent_event (crates/phux-server/src/terminal_actor/mod.rs:1086-1117) so both attached and unattached SUBSCRIBE_EVENTS (0x41) subscribers receive it, exactly as bell/title/idle do today (tests/agent_events.rs). This is the linchpin deliverable: once merged, phux-mobile and phux-web can consume real agent-asked events. Add a wire integration test mirroring tests/agent_events.rs asserting a subscriber receives the agent-asked event.

bead.acceptance_criteria: A server build emits the agent-asked event to subscribers on transition to ASKED; wire integration test green for both attached and unattached subscribers; idempotent on redraw (fires once per genuine new ask, not per output burst).
bead.notes: Unblocks the phux-mobile bridge-consume and pin-bump beads (cross-tracker).
