subject: phux-2sl6.1
message: Decide the agent-asked wire mechanism (new AgentEvent vs TERMINAL_EVENT vs RUN_HOOK)

bead.title: Decide the agent-asked wire mechanism (new AgentEvent vs TERMINAL_EVENT vs RUN_HOOK)
bead.description: The emission mechanism is genuinely unsettled (a real protocol decision, not readable from source). Candidates: (a) a new additive AgentEvent variant Asked{ id, question, suggestions?, ... } on the existing 0xB3 stream -- cheapest, forward-compatible; (b) implement the spec-only TERMINAL_EVENT 0xB1 frame + TerminalEventBody::USER_NOTIFICATION (OSC 9 / OSC 777) at docs/spec/L1.md:183-194; (c) implement the spec-only RUN_HOOK command (L1.md:436, tag 0x06 reserved in frame.rs:403-410) as an agent-hook dispatch point. The downstream Swift consumer already expects agentAsked(terminalID, AgentQuestion{ id, text, suggestions[], waitingSeconds }) (phux-mobile Packages/PhuxKit/Sources/PhuxProtocol/Model.swift:22-45) -- the chosen payload must carry that shape. Resolve whether OSC 9/777 are currently parsed-and-dropped (L1.md:228 "server does not forward every OSC type") or forwardable.
bead.design: Read docs/spec/proto.md:203-213 (versioning), L1.md sec 3.3 and 5.1. Prefer the additive AgentEvent variant unless USER_NOTIFICATION/OSC reuse is clearly superior.
bead.acceptance_criteria: An ADR is written and merged in phux choosing exactly one mechanism with rationale, defining the wire payload (fields + tags) and the minor-version bump, AND this spike FILES the downstream implementation beads (the protocol-type bead and, if 0xB1/RUN_HOOK is chosen, the frame-implementation bead) under this epic with the decided shape.
bead.notes:
Bead-that-creates-beads for the wire shape. Do not pre-write the impl beads here; emit them once the mechanism is chosen.
DECISION MADE + IMPLEMENTED (not yet written as a formal phux ADR): chose the additive AgentEvent::Asked variant on the 0xB3 EVENT stream (tag 0x08) over TERMINAL_EVENT/RUN_HOOK. Shipped in bdb64f6 (feat/agent-asked-wire). Remaining: write the ADR doc to close the spike formally.

subject: phux-2sl6.2
message: Determine how the server detects an agent has entered ASKED

bead.title: Determine how the server detects an agent has entered ASKED
bead.description: Independent of the wire mechanism, the server must DECIDE that an agent is asking. Three clean-room-reusable patterns from herdr (AGPL-3.0 -- PATTERN ONLY, NO source copy): (a) opt-in agent lifecycle hooks reporting state over a channel (authoritative when live), (b) per-agent screen-scrape manifests (evidence gates over OSC title + bottom grid lines), (c) OSC title/progress. phux already owns the PTYs server-side via libghostty, so scrape is feasible. This needs a build/run to confirm what OSC sequences real agents (Claude Code, Codex, pi) actually emit when blocking on input, and which detection layer phux wants to own first. Requires an ADR because it closes off a design space (hook contract vs manifest authority vs both).
bead.design: Clean-room: reuse only the design pattern (hook-authority-wins, scrape-fallback, suppress-on-exit). Author phux own manifests/contract from scratch.
bead.acceptance_criteria: An ADR is written and merged in phux choosing the detection source(s) and (if hooks) the namespaced hook/OSC contract agents implement, with an explicit clean-room note that no AGPL herdr source/manifests/hook scripts are copied. The spike empirically records what at least Claude Code and one other agent emit on block, AND FILES the implementation bead(s) for the chosen detection module under this epic.
bead.notes:
AGPL boundary: herdr src/detect/manifests/*.toml and src/integration/assets/* are off-limits to copy. Pattern reuse is fine.
v1 ask-trigger SHIPPED as a clean-room phux-ask terminal-title sentinel (phux-ask[id]:question?s=a|b|c parsed via strip_prefix; NOT herdr's manifest regex). Full agent-state detection (per-agent manifests and/or opt-in hooks) + the detection ADR remain — see 2sl6.4.

subject: phux-1hnw
message: Stage-2 APNs relay + device-token registry for background agent-asked push

bead.title: Stage-2 APNs relay + device-token registry for background agent-asked push
bead.description: Layer-B background push (ADR-0013 Stage-2, phux-mobile ADR-0015) needs a phux-owned service that holds the wire on behalf of suspended iOS devices and pushes the agent-asked event via APNs, plus a device-token registry keyed by customer. The phux-mobile client half is DONE and inert, waiting on this: PushRegistrar captures the APNs token and would register it here; RelayPush parses a userInfo payload under the 'phux' key (host/pane/agent/question/action). This bead owns the relay service, the device-token registry, and the APNs push payload contract (the key names RelayPush parses are agreed here).
bead.acceptance_criteria: A phux service registers device tokens per customer and, on an agent-asked event for a customer's session, pushes an APNs alert with the host/pane/agent/question payload RelayPush expects; phux-mobile (kfa.8 client) delivers and routes it. Aligns with the ADR-0013 account staging.
bead.notes: Consumer ready: phux-mobile-kfa.8 (client) closed/inert. aps-environment is 'development' on the client until this ships. Coordinate the push payload key names with phux-mobile RelayPush.

subject: phux-84pb
message: Render Kitty graphics on the client (ADR-0034)

bead.title: Render Kitty graphics on the client (ADR-0034)
bead.description: The client ingests TERMINAL_OUTPUT via vt_write then re-renders CELLS ONLY (crates/phux-client/src/attach/render.rs), so out-of-band Kitty graphics images are silently dropped. The server already forwards APC _G bytes verbatim (crates/phux-server/src/downsample.rs) and libghostty-vt exposes the needed APIs (kitty_graphics()/PlacementIterator/is_virtual in crates/libghostty-vt/src/kitty/graphics.rs) but phux never calls them. The pixel-size probe blocker was fixed in PR #138; this is the remaining graphics-rendering work.
bead.design: Per ADR-0034: prefer the Kitty Unicode-placeholder protocol (placements become cell content, fitting the existing cell-walker) with a client-side APC placement re-emit as fallback. The TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT synthesizer (crates/phux-server/src/grid/synthesizer.rs) must replay live image state so images survive detach/reattach.
bead.acceptance_criteria: kitty +kitten icat renders an image inside a phux pane; the image survives detach/reattach; works with the cell renderer (no raw-byte passthrough that corrupts cursor/coords).

subject: phux-npb3
message: Per-pane mouse opt-out (set-pane mouse off) + drag-resize hardening (ADR-0035)

bead.title: Per-pane mouse opt-out (set-pane mouse off) + drag-resize hardening (ADR-0035)
bead.description: Drag-to-resize panes shipped in PR #142 with mouse-on by default and only a GLOBAL 'mouse = false' opt-out ([defaults] in crates/phux-config). ADR-0035 records the per-pane 'set-pane mouse off' escape hatch (documented in docs/consumers/tui.md §7) as follow-up: it needs a 'set-pane' verb plus per-pane client mouse state so one pane wanting raw outer bytes does not force the whole session to mouse=false.
bead.acceptance_criteria: A single pane can disable mouse capture while sibling panes keep drag-resize; a second press during an active drag is handled cleanly (no stale grab).
bead.notes: Two smaller hardening items from the PR #142 review: (1) a second Press arriving while a divider drag is already active falls through to normal routing without clearing the stale grab — handle defensively; (2) ?1003h any-motion tracking is intentionally NOT enabled, so inner apps wanting bare hover-motion (no button held) get nothing — revisit only if a real consumer needs it.
